title: Review & setlists: David Lee Roth and Sammy Hagar both hit Greater Boston. Who rocked harder?
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: Sammy Hagar performed a 19-song set at Xfinity Center in Mansfield, June 24, showcasing hits from his Van Halen tenure.
David Lee Roth delivered an 18-song set at House of Blues in Boston, May 21, focusing on classic Van Halen tracks.
Joe Satriani and Michael Anthony enhanced Hagar's performance, while Roth's set was bolstered by backup singers.

title: How did it get this bad for the Red Sox, and where should they go from here?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox rank last in MLB with a 32-46 record, suffering a series loss to the Colorado Rockies.
Injuries and underperforming young players have contributed to the Red Sox's struggles this season.
Moving forward, the Red Sox aim to add veteran power hitters and improve the bullpen.

title: Boston has one of the best free attractions in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail ranked No. 7 among USA Today's 10 best free attractions in America.
The 2.5-mile Freedom Trail connects 16 historical sites from Boston Common to Bunker Hill Monument.
The No. 1 free attraction in America is the Saint Louis Zoo in St. Louis.

title: ‘Until next time Boston’: Scottish newspaper ad thanks city for welcoming Tartan Army
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Scottish soccer fans, known as the Tartan Army, expressed gratitude to Boston with a full-page ad in The Herald.
Boston embraced Scottish fans during their World Cup visit, participating in local events and fostering positive interactions.
Mayor Michelle Wu plans to designate Glasgow as a sister city to Boston, enhancing their cultural and civic ties.

title: A 3-bedroom 1800s Colonial in Marblehead — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 3-bedroom New England Colonial built in 1842 is for sale in Marblehead.
The 2,136 square foot home includes 2 full bathrooms and one half bathroom.
Property taxes for the home are $11,359 in 2026.

title: After a tense process, City Council accepts Wu’s amended budget. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council accepted Mayor Michelle Wu's amended $4.9 billion budget proposal, ending a contentious budget season.
The City Council reallocated $11.8 million in amendments, including restoring funds for rental vouchers, youth jobs, and senior programs.
Tensions arose from initial budget rejections, protests, and disagreements over personnel cuts, highlighting ongoing financial challenges for Boston.

title: Metal manufacturer to build third Massachusetts plant, bringing over 1,000 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: VulcanForms to expand with a third facility in Devens, Massachusetts, creating over 1,000 jobs.
The expansion, aided by a $21.26 million state tax credit, involves a new one-million-square-foot facility.
VulcanForms produces parts for various industries using metal 3D printing and automation technologies.

title: July 4 fireworks in Massachusetts (2026): Full list of towns, times, and rain dates
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Over 100 fireworks celebrations scheduled in Massachusetts through mid-July, with permits expected to increase.
Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular marks its 52nd anniversary on July 4, hosted by Jane Lynch.
Fireworks include events for July 4, block parties, sports games, and feasts across Massachusetts.

title: Driver dead in wrong-way crash in Northborough
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: One person died and another was injured in a wrong-way crash on I-290 in Northborough.
A Subaru and a Jeep collided head-on, with the Subaru's driver pronounced dead at the scene.
The crash is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police and Worcester County District Attorney's Office.

title: More of Michael Proctor’s slur-filled messages revealed in bid to dismiss 2021 murder case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Proctor's racist texts were revealed in a motion to dismiss Myles King's 2021 murder charge.
Proctor's messages included racial slurs and derogatory remarks, affecting the integrity of the investigation.
The public defender agency has called for a review of all cases involving Proctor and a former Canton officer.
